subheading: Run Google's Nano Banana Pro, the Gemini 3 Pro Image model, in ComfyUI: high-fidelity generation, multi-image fusion, and 4K output, on Comfy Cloud.
extendedDescription:
Most people meet Nano Banana Pro comfyui workflows through one job: take several reference images and fuse them into one clean, high-fidelity result. Nano Banana Pro is Google DeepMind's flagship image model, the Gemini 3 Pro Image build, and it is a cloud model reached through ComfyUI's API nodes rather than a local checkpoint. The workflows on this page wire those calls into editable graphs, so the prompt, the reference images, and the output flow through nodes you can chain.

What makes it stand out is the combination of world knowledge and control. It can pull on Google's knowledge to render real-world subjects accurately, blend up to fourteen reference images into a single coherent output, and hold a character consistent across separate generations. Text rendering is a strength too, with clean lettering and translation across languages, and native output reaches 4K, up to 4096 by 4096, for work that has to hold up at size.

The family has a faster tier as well. Gemini 2.5 Flash Image, the original Nano Banana, trades some fidelity for speed and cost, which suits interactive editing loops, while Nano Banana Pro is the one to reach for when the result has to be production-ready. Both run through the same API nodes, so a workflow can target whichever fits the task.

Because these are open ComfyUI graphs around a hosted model, you control the inputs that shape the output: the prompt, the reference images, the aspect ratio, and how the result feeds the rest of your pipeline. To run one, open a Nano Banana Pro workflow on Comfy Cloud with nothing to install, add your prompt and reference images, and generate. Re-run with adjusted references until the fusion and detail land.

modelSpec.summary: Nano Banana Pro is Google DeepMind's flagship image model, internally Gemini 3 Pro Image, accessed through ComfyUI API nodes. It fuses up to 14 references, renders 4K output, and holds character consistency.

howToUse: Pick a Nano Banana Pro workflow: generation, multi-image fusion, or editing.
Open it on Comfy Cloud, which runs the ComfyUI API nodes that call the model.
Write your prompt, and upload the reference images you want to blend.
Choose the aspect ratio and the tier, Pro for fidelity or Flash for speed.
Generate the result and review the fusion, detail, and any rendered text.
Re-run with adjusted references or prompt, then download the image.

faqItems.question: What are Nano Banana Pro ComfyUI workflows?
faqItems.answer: They are ready-made ComfyUI graphs that run Google's Nano Banana Pro, the Gemini 3 Pro Image model, through ComfyUI's API nodes. Each wires up a task like multi-image fusion or prompt-based editing, so you give a prompt and reference images and get a result without building the graph yourself.

faqItems.question: Can I run Nano Banana Pro locally on my own GPU?
faqItems.answer: No. Nano Banana Pro is a hosted Google model with no public weights, so it runs through ComfyUI's API nodes rather than on local hardware. Comfy Cloud handles the compute, so no powerful GPU is needed on your end.

faqItems.question: How many reference images can Nano Banana Pro use?
faqItems.answer: It can blend up to fourteen reference images into a single coherent output. That high reference count is what makes it strong for fusing subjects, styles, and scenes that other models can only take one or two inputs for.

faqItems.question: What resolution can Nano Banana Pro generate?
faqItems.answer: Nano Banana Pro generates natively at up to 4K, up to 4096 by 4096 pixels. That makes it suitable for work that has to hold up at large sizes, such as posters or detailed product visuals.

faqItems.question: What is the difference between Nano Banana and Nano Banana Pro?
faqItems.answer: Nano Banana Pro is the Gemini 3 Pro Image build, tuned for high fidelity, 4K output, and multi-image fusion. The original Nano Banana, Gemini 2.5 Flash Image, is the faster and lower-cost tier that suits quick interactive editing.

faqItems.question: Can Nano Banana Pro keep a character consistent?
faqItems.answer: Yes. It can hold a subject's identity consistent across separate generations, so a character looks the same from one image to the next, with identity preserved for up to five people in a scene. That makes it useful for sets of related visuals rather than one-off renders.

faqItems.question: Do I need to install anything to run these workflows?
faqItems.answer: No. Open the workflow on Comfy Cloud in your browser and the API nodes call the model for you, with nothing to download or set up. You can also run the same graphs in a local ComfyUI install that has API node support, since the model itself is hosted either way.

faqItems.question: How is Nano Banana Pro billed in ComfyUI?
faqItems.answer: Comfy Cloud is a paid plan, and the API nodes that call Nano Banana Pro draw from your subscription credits per run. The exact cost of a generation depends on the tier you pick and the parameters you set, so the faster Gemini 2.5 Flash Image tier costs less per call than the Pro tier.
